description: Collapse the eleven cmd/* binaries into two: a single `strata` binary with subcommands (CockroachDB-shape: `strata server`, `strata version`, ...) and a separate `strata-admin` operator CLI. Every background worker (gc, lifecycle, notify, replicator, access-log, inventory, audit-export, manifest-rewriter) becomes a goroutine inside `strata server`, selected by --workers / STRATA_WORKERS, each holding its own internal/leader lease keyed on the worker name. The one-shot SSE key rotation moves into `strata-admin rewrap`. Hard cut, no symlinks, no thin wrappers. End state: two binaries, one Docker image, one Kubernetes Deployment per replica.

userStories.title: Roadmap maintenance rule in project CLAUDE.md
userStories.description: As a developer or autonomous agent doing feature work on Strata, I need an explicit, durable rule in the project root CLAUDE.md that any commit which closes or discovers a ROADMAP.md item must update the roadmap in the same commit, so the roadmap stays an honest reflection of project state at every SHA. Lands first so all subsequent stories follow the rule.
acceptanceCriteria: New top-level section `## Roadmap maintenance` appended to project root CLAUDE.md (the file at the repo root, NOT scripts/ralph/CLAUDE.md)

Rule states: every commit that closes a ROADMAP.md item MUST flip the bullet to the format `~~**P<n> — <title>.**~~ — **Done.** <one-line summary>. (commit `<sha>`)` in the same commit, or the immediate follow-up if the closing SHA is needed inline
Rule states: every commit that discovers a new gap, latent bug, or regression MUST add a new entry under the appropriate ROADMAP.md section (P1/P2/P3 by severity, or `Known latent bugs` for bugs)
Rule applies to all work — Ralph autorun and human-driven commits alike
Section includes one explicit example each of close-flip and new-discovery diff shapes so a junior reader knows the exact pattern
Rule notes ROADMAP.md is the canonical project state list; PRDs in tasks/ are scoped to specific cycles and do not need to mirror the roadmap
No code or test changes (docs-only story)
git diff CLAUDE.md shows only the new section added; no unrelated edits
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Worker registry + leader-elected goroutine pattern + panic recovery
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a cmd/strata/workers/ package that registers workers by name and runs each in its own leader-elected goroutine with panic recovery + exponential-backoff restart, so a single worker panic never kills the gateway or sibling workers.
acceptanceCriteria: New cmd/strata/workers/registry.go with `type Worker struct { Name string; Build func(deps Dependencies) (Runner, error) }` and a `Register(w Worker)` function
Dependencies carries *slog.Logger, meta.Store, data.Backend, *otel.Provider, prometheus.Registerer, plus per-worker env hooks
Each registered worker runs in its own goroutine with its own leader.Session keyed on the worker name (gc-leader, lifecycle-leader, ...)
Loss-of-lease cancels only that worker's context, never the gateway's or another worker's

PANIC RECOVERY: each worker goroutine wraps Run(ctx) in `defer recover()`. Caught panic logs ERROR with stack trace, increments `strata_worker_panic_total{worker=<name>}` Prometheus counter, releases the leader lease, then re-acquires + restarts the worker after a backoff
Restart backoff is exponential: 1s -> 5s -> 30s -> 2m cap; resets to 1s after the worker stays up >= 5 minutes. Backoff function injectable so tests flatten it to zero
Process must NOT exit on a worker panic; gateway and sibling workers must continue serving
--workers= parses comma-separated names into a deduplicated set; unknown names cause immediate startup error
STRATA_WORKERS env var sets the same list; flag overrides env

Unit tests cover: registry round-trip, unknown name rejection, dependency injection, lease loss isolation, panic-then-restart cycle (inject panic in fake worker -> verify counter increments, lease released, worker restarts within backoff, gateway + sibling workers untouched)
go test ./cmd/strata/workers/... passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Move rewrap into strata-admin as a subcommand
userStories.description: As an operator, I want `strata-admin rewrap` to run the SSE master key rotation worker, since it is a one-shot human-invoked operation rather than a long-running daemon.
acceptanceCriteria: cmd/strata-admin/rewrap.go adds a `rewrap` subcommand wiring up internal/rewrap exactly as cmd/strata-rewrap/main.go does today
--target-key-id <id> flag selects the destination wrap key; defaults match the env-driven flow
Logs progress to stderr; exits 0 when every bucket completes; exits non-zero with structured error if a bucket fails
No --continue / --from-bucket flags — resumption is automatic via the persisted per-bucket progress rows (meta.Store.SetRewrapProgress)

Idempotency preserved (re-run skips already-rewrapped rows) — covered by an existing or new integration test
